openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsyn.purpose: Provides a typed snapshot of subscription-related billing state already in the DB (invoice lines, split-line hierarchies, flat-fee and usage-based charges) keyed by ChildUniqueReferenceID, so the subscription-sync reconciler can diff persisted vs target state without re-querying inside the diff loop.

patterns.name: Sealed Item type hierarchy
patterns.description: All four concrete types (persistedLine, persistedSplitLineHierarchy, persistedUsageBasedCharge, persistedFlatFeeCharge) implement Item but are package-private; callers use exported ItemAs* helpers returning typed errors.
patterns.example: line, err := persistedstate.ItemAsLine(item) // error if item is not line-backed

patterns.name: Construction-time validation via private constructors
patterns.description: Private constructors (newPersistedLine, etc.) call Validate()/nil-check before returning. Public entry points are NewItemFromLineOrHierarchy and NewChargeItemFromChargeType.
patterns.example: item, err := newPersistedUsageBasedCharge(charge) // calls charge.Validate() inside

patterns.name: MinimumWindowSizeDuration normalization on load
patterns.description: normalizePersistedLineOrHierarchy and normalizeSubscriptionReference truncate timestamps to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration on read; apply to every line/hierarchy from GetLinesForSubscription.

patterns.name: Unified ByUniqueID map
patterns.description: State.ByUniqueID merges lines/hierarchies and charges into one map keyed by ChildUniqueReferenceID; duplicate keys are a load-time error.
patterns.example: if _, ok := byUniqueID[uniqueID]; ok { return State{}, fmt.Errorf("duplicate unique ids") }

patterns.name: Nil chargeService guard
patterns.description: Loader.loadChargesForSubscription returns an empty map when chargeService is nil, allowing construction without charges enabled.
patterns.example: if l.chargeService == nil { return map[string]Item{}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: Item interface, four concrete impls, typed getter interfaces, and public factories NewItemFromLineOrHierarchy / NewChargeItemFromChargeType.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each concrete type has a var _ Item = ... guard. A new item type needs all Item methods, a matching ItemAs* helper, and a NewChargeItemFromChargeType case. Credit-purchase charges are explicitly rejected.

key_file_guides.role: Loads state via billingService.GetLinesForSubscription and chargeService.ListCharges, normalizes timestamps, assembles unified State.
key_file_guides.watch_for: normalizePersistedLineOrHierarchy must apply to every loaded line; subscription-tied credit-purchase charges return an error and must never be silently ignored.

key_file_guides.role: State and Invoices types with Validate; Invoices.IsGatheringInvoice is the only query method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: State.Validate errors if either map is nil — always build via LoadForSubscription, never hand-construct State{}.

anti_patterns: Constructing a concrete persistedXxx struct directly instead of via the private constructors / factories.
Skipping normalizePersistedLineOrHierarchy on load — sub-second timestamps cause phantom no-op repairs.
Accessing backing data via direct type assertion instead of ItemAs* helpers.
Adding credit-purchase charges to a subscription's persisted state — explicitly unsupported, must error.
Hand-constructing State{} with nil maps — Validate() rejects it.

decisions.decision: Item interface with private concrete types and public ItemAs* accessors.
decisions.rationale: Keeps construction-time validation inside the package and forces callers to handle the wrong-type error path, preventing silent nil-dereferences in the reconciler.

decisions.decision: Timestamp normalization on read rather than at write time.
decisions.rationale: Historical rows carry sub-second precision; normalizing on load avoids a migration while ensuring diffs compare at MinimumWindowSizeDuration resolution.

code_examples.scenario: Load persisted state and extract a line

openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsyn.purpose: Computes the desired (target) set of billing line items for a subscription as of a point in time — iterating subscription phases and rate-card cadences via PhaseIterator, then correcting period boundaries against persisted state — so the reconciler knows exactly what should exist in the DB.

patterns.name: PhaseIterator per-phase generation
patterns.description: collectUpcomingLines creates one PhaseIterator per phase, advancing through aligned billing periods up to generationLimit (never past the current period's end). HasInvoicableItems() gates empty/zero-length phases.
patterns.example: iterator, err := NewPhaseIterator(b.logger, b.tracer, subs, phase.SubscriptionPhase.Key); if !iterator.HasInvoicableItems() { continue }; items, err := iterator.Generate(ctx, generationLimit)

patterns.name: UniqueID composite key
patterns.description: Every generated item carries UniqueID = subID/phaseKey/itemKey/v[N]/period[N] joined by '/'. This MUST match the ChildUniqueReferenceID format in persistedstate so the diff can correlate.
patterns.example: UniqueID: strings.Join([]string{sub.ID, phase.Spec.PhaseKey, item.Spec.ItemKey, fmt.Sprintf("v[%d]", version), fmt.Sprintf("period[%d]", periodIdx)}, "/")

patterns.name: MinimumWindowSizeDuration truncation of periods
patterns.description: truncateItemsIfNeeded truncates ServicePeriod, BillingPeriod, FullServicePeriod on every item; non-flat-fee items with empty service periods after truncation are dropped.

patterns.name: iterationEnd end-of-second extension
patterns.description: Generate() truncates iterationEnd then adds (MinimumWindowSizeDuration - time.Nanosecond) so the last window is fully included. Never pass raw asOf as iterationEnd.

patterns.name: Period-start continuity from persisted state
patterns.description: correctPeriodStartForUpcomingLines aligns a period's start to the persisted previous period's end when it carries AnnotationSubscriptionSyncIgnore + ForceContinuousLines; the boundary is truncated to MinimumWindowSizeDuration.

patterns.name: GetExpectedLine returns nil (not error) for zero-amount
patterns.description: StateItem.GetExpectedLine returns (nil, nil) when a pro-rated flat-fee amount rounds to zero — meaning 'do not create'. Use GetExpectedLineOrErr only when a line is mandatory.
patterns.example: line, err := item.GetExpectedLine(); if err != nil { return err }; if line == nil { /* skip — zero amount */ }

patterns.name: tracex.Start spans on public methods
patterns.description: Non-trivial Builder/PhaseIterator methods wrap their body in tracex.Start for OTel tracing of generation timing.
patterns.example: span := tracex.Start[State](ctx, b.tracer, "...targetstate.Build"); return span.Wrap(func(ctx) (State, error) { ... })

key_file_guides.role: Builder.Build entry point: validates input, caps view at customer deletion, iterates phases, corrects period starts, returns assembled State.
key_file_guides.watch_for: generationLimit logic advances to currBillingPeriod.To unless asOf precedes subscription start or phase hasn't started. Customer-deleted-at capping (withActiveTo) is the only sanctioned guard against generating past the customer lifecycle.

key_file_guides.role: Generates SubscriptionItemWithPeriods for billable items in one phase (recurring, one-time, aligned periods, truncation).
key_file_guides.watch_for: maxSafeIter=1000 guards infinite loops. Extend iterationEnd to end-of-second before generateAligned. Preserve the UniqueID composite format exactly.

key_file_guides.role: StateItem with GetExpectedLine, IsBillable, shouldProrate and price dispatch.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetExpectedLine returns (nil, nil) for zero-amount flat fee — check nil separately from error. shouldProrate returns false when the subscription ends within the service period (avoids over-proration on cancellation).

key_file_guides.role: Table-driven testify/suite tests for aligned/unaligned cadences, phase boundaries, version splits, truncation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New cases append to tcs and call suite.Run; expected Key UniqueID format must match phaseiterator.go exactly.

anti_patterns: Passing raw asOf as iterationEnd — must extend to end-of-second first.
Constructing UniqueID with a different separator or field order than subID/phaseKey/itemKey/v[N]/period[N].
Returning an error from GetExpectedLine when the line is nil — nil means 'do not create'.
Skipping correctPeriodStartForUpcomingLines — continuity annotations get ignored and spurious repairs proposed.
Generating lines past the customer's deleted-at — withActiveTo capping is the only sanctioned guard.

decisions.decision: PhaseIterator generates target state purely from the subscription view without DB access.
decisions.rationale: Stateless generation makes the target-vs-persisted diff a pure in-memory computation, idempotent and safe to retry.

decisions.decision: correctPeriodStartForUpcomingLines patches boundaries from persisted state.
decisions.rationale: When a previous period was manually frozen (SyncIgnore + ForceContinuousLines), the next period must start exactly where it ended regardless of cadence-computed start.

decisions.decision: GetExpectedLine returns nil for zero-amount lines instead of pre-filtering.
decisions.rationale: Separating 'nothing to bill' (nil) from 'error' lets the reconciler distinguish legitimate zero-value periods from failures without a mandatory IsBillable pre-check.

code_examples.scenario: Build target state and iterate billable items
